001package guiNewAccount;
002
003import java.sql.SQLException;
004
005import applicationMain.ValidationUtil;
006import database.Database;
007import entityClasses.User;
008
009/*******
010 * <p> Title: ControllerNewAccount Class. </p>
011 * 
012 * <p> Description: The Java/FX-based New Account Page.  This class provides the controller actions
013 * to allow the user to establish a new account after responding to an invitation and the use of a
014 * one time code.
015 * 
016 * The controller deals with the user pressing the "User Step" button widget being click.  If also
017 * supports the user click on the "Quit" button widget.
018 * 
019 * The class has been written assuming that the View or the Model are the only class methods that
020 * can invoke these methods.  This is why each has been declared at "protected".  Do not change any
021 * of these methods to public.</p>
022 * 
023 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
024 * 
025 * @author Lynn Robert Carter
026 * 
027 * @version 1.00                2025-08-17 Initial version
028 *  
029 */
030
031public class ControllerNewAccount {
032        
033        /*-********************************************************************************************
034
035        The User Interface Actions for this page
036        
037        This controller is not a class that gets instantiated.  Rather, it is a collection of protected
038        static methods that can be called by the View (which is a singleton instantiated object) and 
039        the Model is often just a stub, or will be a singleton instantiated object.
040        
041        */
042
043        /**
044         * Default constructor is not used.
045         */
046        public ControllerNewAccount() {
047        }
048        
049        
050        // Reference for the in-memory database so this package has access
051        private static Database theDatabase = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.database;
052        
053        /**********
054         * <p> Method: public doCreateUser() </p>
055         * 
056         * <p> Description: This method is called when the user has clicked on the User Setup
057         * button.  This method checks the input fields to see that they are valid.  If so, it then
058         * creates the account by adding information to the database.
059         * 
060         * The method reaches batch to the view page and to fetch the information needed rather than
061         * passing that information as parameters.
062         * 
063         */     
064        protected static void doCreateUser() {
065                
066                // Fetch the username and password. (We use the first of the two here, but we will validate
067                // that the two password fields are the same before we do anything with it.)
068                String username = ViewNewAccount.text_Username.getText().trim();
069                String password = ViewNewAccount.text_Password1.getText();
070                
071                String usernameError = ValidationUtil.validateAsuUserId(username);
072                if (usernameError != null) {
073                        ViewNewAccount.alertUsernamePasswordError.setContentText(usernameError);
074                        ViewNewAccount.alertUsernamePasswordError.showAndWait();
075                        return;
076                }
077                
078                String passwordError = ValidationUtil.validatePassword(password);
079                if (passwordError != null) {
080                        ViewNewAccount.alertUsernamePasswordError.setContentText(passwordError);
081                        ViewNewAccount.alertUsernamePasswordError.showAndWait();
082                        return;
083                }
084                
085                String password2 = ViewNewAccount.text_Password2.getText();
086                if (password2 == null || password2.isEmpty()) {
087                        ViewNewAccount.alertUsernamePasswordError.setContentText("Password is required.");
088                        ViewNewAccount.alertUsernamePasswordError.showAndWait();
089                        return;
090                }
091                
092                // Display key information to the log
093                System.out.println("** Account for Username: " + username + "; theInvitationCode: "+
094                                ViewNewAccount.theInvitationCode + "; email address: " + 
095                                ViewNewAccount.emailAddress + "; Role: " + ViewNewAccount.theRole);
096                
097                // Initialize local variables that will be created during this process
098                int roleCode = 0;
099                User user = null;
100
101                // Make sure the two passwords are the same.    
102                if (ViewNewAccount.text_Password1.getText().
103                                compareTo(ViewNewAccount.text_Password2.getText()) == 0) {
104                        
105                        // The passwords match so we will set up the role and the User object base on the 
106                        // information provided in the invitation
107                        if (ViewNewAccount.theRole.compareTo("Admin") == 0) {
108                                roleCode = 1;
109                                user = new User(username, password, "", "", "", "", "", true, false, false);
110                        } else if (ViewNewAccount.theRole.compareTo("Role1") == 0) {
111                                roleCode = 2;
112                                user = new User(username, password, "", "", "", "", "", false, true, false);
113                        } else if (ViewNewAccount.theRole.compareTo("Role2") == 0) {
114                                roleCode = 3;
115                                user = new User(username, password, "", "", "", "", "", false, false, true);
116                        } else {
117                                System.out.println(
118                                                "**** Trying to create a New Account for a role that does not exist!");
119                                System.exit(0);
120                        }
121                        
122                        // Unlike the FirstAdmin, we know the email address, so set that into the user as well.
123                user.setEmailAddress(ViewNewAccount.emailAddress);
124
125                // Inform the system about which role will be played
126                        applicationMain.FoundationsMain.activeHomePage = roleCode;
127                        
128                // Create the account based on user and proceed to the user account update page
129            try {
130                // Create a new User object with the pre-set role and register in the database
131                theDatabase.register(user);
132            } catch (SQLException e) {
133                System.err.println("*** ERROR *** Database error: " + e.getMessage());
134                e.printStackTrace();
135                System.exit(0);
136            }
137            
138            // The account has been set, so remove the invitation from the system
139            theDatabase.removeInvitationAfterUse(
140                        ViewNewAccount.text_Invitation.getText());
141            
142            // Set the database so it has this user and the current user
143            theDatabase.getUserAccountDetails(username);
144
145            // Navigate to the Welcome Login Page
146            guiUserUpdate.ViewUserUpdate.displayUserUpdate(ViewNewAccount.theStage, user);
147                }
148                else {
149                        // The two passwords are NOT the same, so clear the passwords, explain the passwords
150                        // must be the same, and clear the message as soon as the first character is typed.
151                        ViewNewAccount.text_Password1.setText("");
152                        ViewNewAccount.text_Password2.setText("");
153                        ViewNewAccount.alertUsernamePasswordError.showAndWait();
154                }
155        }
156
157        
158        /**********
159         * <p> Method: public performQuit() </p>
160         * 
161         * <p> Description: This method is called when the user has clicked on the Quit button.  Doing
162         * this terminates the execution of the application.  All important data must be stored in the
163         * database, so there is no cleanup required.  (This is important so we can minimize the impact
164         * of crashed.)
165         * 
166         */     
167        protected static void performQuit() {
168                System.out.println("Perform Quit");
169                System.exit(0);
170        }       
171}
